description: Public appeal of a blind woman to the congregation of Fustat to pay the fee of 4 dinars charged by a Muslim physician for the treatment of either herself or her sick daughter, who suffered from dropsy (istisqā'). Her other children (or child) had been given as security for this sum. Probably written by Hillel b. Eli (dated documents 1066–1108). Information from Goitein's note card. See also Med Soc, II, Appendix C, #96 (p. 501), and Med Soc I, p. 259 on children as collateral. ASE.
tags: medical charity, disability: women's, disability: vision, illness: dropsy, illness: cost, Illness, disability, Appeal, Illness: physician, illness letter 969-1517, Illness: women's
description_he: פנייה ציבורית של אישה עיוורת לקהל פוסטאט לשלם 4 דינרים לרופא מוסלמי עבור טיפול בה או בבתה החולה; ילדיה ניתנו כבטוחה
